meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Commercial flights from Tehran’s main airport resume amid cautious normalcy
items.summary: Flights are gradually back in the air, but uncertainty on the ground still lingers.

items.title: Ukraine begins to flex muscle as an emerging air power, angering Russia
items.summary: Russian officials cast Ukrainian attacks as 'terrorism' as they urge 'vigilance'.

items.title: UK police charge man over stabbing of two Jewish men in London
items.summary: British officials have raised the national terrorism threat to its second-highest level.

items.title: Iran vows ‘long, painful’ response if US renews attacks
items.summary: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict have hit an impasse, with a ceasefire in place since April 8.

items.title: Russia hammers targets across Ukraine overnight
items.summary: Attacks on towns on the Black Sea and in central Ukraine kill one, injure several.

items.title: What do Ukraine’s robot soldiers mean for the future of warfare?
items.summary: Remote-controlled weapons have been used for some time, but AI is now on the cusp of making battlefield decisions.

items.title: On May Day, Gaza’s workers find whatever source of income they can
items.summary: Gaza's economic collapse leaves workers in perilous jobs, with unemployment soaring amid ongoing devastation.

items.title: Why is piracy rising off Somalia again — and is the Iran war responsible?
items.summary: At least four vessels have been hijacked in the past week or two, raising fears of a piracy resurgence.

items.title: Timberwolves eliminate Nuggets as Knicks demolish Hawks in NBA playoffs
items.summary: Meanwhile, the Celtics-76ers playoff heads for a decisive Game 7 after Philadelphia beat Boston 106-93.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
items.summary: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

items.title: Trump backs FIFA letting Iran play at World Cup in US
items.summary: After months of campaigns and questions, FIFA says Iran will compete in the World Cup and matches will remain in the US.

items.title: New Banksy sculpture appears to show politician blinded by his own flag
items.summary: Banksy has unveiled a new sculpture of a man stepping off a stone base with his face obscured by a flag.

items.title: Condemnation and protests against Israel’s raid of Gaza aid flotilla
items.summary: Israel has provoked international condemnation for its interception of Gaza-bound aid boats in international waters.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US ports siege ‘intolerable’; Trump mulls action
items.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least 2,586 people and wounded more than 8,000 since March 2, local media report.

items.title: Arsenal vs Fulham: Premier League – teams, start, lineups, title race
items.summary: Arsenal will move six points clear of EPL title rivals Man City, who will have two games in hand, if they beat Fulham.

items.title: Palestine FA chief hits out at Israel federation VP at FIFA Congress
items.summary: Palestine and Israel representatives had been lined up close together at the FIFA Congress in Canada.

items.title: First US-Venezuela flight lands in Caracas after seven-year suspension
items.summary: American Airlines has resumed flights as Donald Trump moves to rebuild ties following the abduction of Nicolas Maduro.

items.title: US gas reaches $4.30 per gallon; Trump says prices will drop after Iran war
items.summary: Price of petrol in US jumps by nearly 30 cents in one week amid Strait of Hormuz blockade and Iran diplomatic deadlock.

items.title: Palestinians mourn football loving teenager killed by Israeli army
items.summary: Mourners in Hebron laid to rest 16-year-old Ibrahim al-Khayatt who was shot in the chest by Israeli soldiers.

items.title: Kenya’s Sawe hailed at State House after historic marathon
items.summary: Kenyan runner Sabastian Sawe received a hero’s welcome from President William Ruto.

items.title: Palestinian journalist released from Israeli prison unrecognisable
items.summary: Palestinian journalist Ali al-Samoudi was released from Israeli prison with severe weight loss, showing signs of torture

items.title: Hegseth testifies on Iran war before Senate committee: Key takeaways
items.summary: Pentagon chief testifies alongside top general for second day, in first public Congressional hearings since war began.

items.title: Flotilla activist: Israel’s ‘brutal attack’ will not deter us
items.summary: Activist Omer Aslan says Israel’s interception of 22 boats late Wednesday will not stop the Global Sumud Flotilla.

items.title: How is Israel replicating its Gaza tactics in Lebanon?
items.summary: Widespread Israeli attacks on civilian targets in Lebanon are a repeated tactic from Gaza.

items.title: Trump says Iran can play at World Cup in US after FIFA confirmation
items.summary: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino has said Iran will play their World Cup matches, leading to agreement from US President.
